channel.title: City Record Digest
channel.description: Notable NYC government notices — procurement, hearings, rules, and more

item.title: Heavy Duty Cleaning, Queens
item.description:
Dept. of Social Svcs/Human Resources Administration award valued at $247K, vendor: Clarisa Guerrero.
Dept. of Social Svcs/Human Resources Administration | Procurement | $247K

item.title: FY26-NY 15/15 Congregate Supportive Housing
item.description:
Health and Mental Hygiene is awarding a $18.0M contract to Unique People Services Inc..
Health and Mental Hygiene | Procurement | $18.0M

item.title: MEDIA BUYING SERVICES
item.description:
Health and Mental Hygiene is awarding a $9.5M contract to OpAD Media Solutions LLC.
Health and Mental Hygiene | Procurement | $9.5M

item.title: Shelter Services for Single Adults
item.description:
Homeless Services intent to award.
Homeless Services | Procurement

item.title: FY27 Renewal - FWC Siena House (27 units)
item.description:
Homeless Services intent to award.
Homeless Services | Procurement

item.title: Shelter Services to Homeless Adult Families
item.description:
Homeless Services intent to award.
Homeless Services | Procurement

item.title: Shelter Facilities for Homeless Families with Children
item.description:
Homeless Services intent to award.
Homeless Services | Procurement

item.title: Mobile Maverick UAS (Unmanned Aerial Systems) mobile command
item.description:
Police Department award valued at $984K, vendor: FlyMotion LLC.
Police Department | Procurement | $984K

item.title: Advance and Earn Negotiated Acquisition Extension
item.description:
Youth and Community Development is awarding a $5.3M contract to NYSARC Inc New York City Chapter through negotiated acquisition — a non-competitive process.
Youth and Community Development | Procurement | $5.3M

item.title: FY26 NAQ NYTS - Youth Team Sports
item.description:
Youth and Community Development award valued at $50K, vendor: Neighborhood Association for Inter-Cultural Affair.
Youth and Community Development | Procurement | $50K

item.title: NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING ON THE DRAFT ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STATEMENT, DEWITT CLINTON PARK NORTH, CEQR NO. 25DCP049M
item.description:
City Planning has posted a public hearings. As a key city agency, its procurement and policy actions are worth monitoring.
City Planning | Public Hearings and Meetings

item.title: Tactical Flashlight Kits for NYPD Police Academy Recruits
item.description:
Police Department award valued at $597K, vendor: Western Reserve Technology LTD.
Police Department | Procurement | $597K

item.title: Shelter Facilities for Homeless Single Adults
item.description:
DHS is extending a $21.5M contract with Samaritan Daytop Village for single-adult shelter operations through June 2027 — a negotiated acquisition with no competitive bidding. Public comments due March 19.
Homeless Services | Public Comment on Contract Awards | $21.5M

item.title: NAE for HELP Social Service Corp. — Franklin Women's Shelter
item.description:
DHS intends to award an $11.4M one-year extension to HELP Social Service Corp. for the Franklin Women's Shelter. A new RFP is anticipated by July 1 — this is a bridge contract. Two large non-competitive shelter contracts on the same day signals the scale of DHS's provider dependency.
Homeless Services | Procurement | $11.4M

item.title: Purchase of EVOQ4 Water Meters — Sole Source
item.description:
DEP is sole-sourcing an $18.7M contract for water meters — the largest single contract in today's Record. Sole-source procurement at this scale raises questions about vendor lock-in for critical infrastructure. Comments due March 24.
Environmental Protection | Public Comment on Contract Awards | $18.7M

item.title: Jobs-Plus Employment Program Concept Paper — Expanding to 62 NYCHA Developments
item.description:
HRA is expanding its Jobs-Plus employment program from 40 to 62 NYCHA developments — a major scale-up of the workforce program serving public housing residents. Information session April 2; comments due April 27.
Dept. of Social Svcs/Human Resources Administration | Special Materials

item.title: Asset Management Portfolio — Decarbonization Consulting
item.description:
HPD awarded $2.5M to Kinetic Communities for a data-driven approach to help building owners comply with NYC climate laws through decarbonization, funded by DOE's EECBG Program. Climate policy meets affordable housing preservation.
Housing Preservation and Development | Procurement | $2.5M

item.title: ELURP Application: 351 Powers Avenue — Affordable Housing Disposition
item.description:
Public hearing on HPD affordable housing project at 351 Powers Avenue in Mott Haven. ELURP applications involve city-owned land disposition for housing development — part of the pipeline for new affordable units. Virtual hearing March 24.
Borough President - Bronx | Public Hearings and Meetings

item.title: PhotoManager Solution — DataWorks Plus
item.description:
NYPD intends to sole-source with DataWorks Plus for its PhotoManager identification photo management system. Surveillance technology procurement always merits attention. Other vendors can submit capability statements by March 25.
Police Department | Procurement

item.title: Enterprise Risk Management and Internal Audit Services
item.description:
The relatively new Public Housing Preservation Trust is seeking enterprise risk management and internal audit services — building out governance infrastructure as it manages the conversion of NYCHA buildings. Proposals due March 26.
Public Housing Preservation Trust | Procurement

item.title: DYCD Fatherhood Implementation Program
item.description:
$4M to Rising Ground Inc. for fatherhood programs serving fathers at or below the poverty level. The largest human services award today.
Youth and Community Development | Procurement | $4.0M

item.title: Union Square Park Café License — 15-Year Concession
item.description:
$3.3M concession to Union Square Parkhouse LLC for café and snack bar kiosks in Union Square Park over 15 years. Guaranteed annual minimums from $150K to $277K plus 6% of gross receipts.
Parks and Recreation | Procurement | $3.3M

item.title: Brand Specific Lifeguard Uniforms — Sole Source
item.description:
$1M sole-source for brand-specific lifeguard uniforms. Three sole-source contracts in a single day's Record is notable.
Parks and Recreation | Procurement | $1.0M

item.title: Manhattan Borough Board — FY27 Preliminary Budget Response
item.description:
Public hearing and vote on the FY27 budget response. Budget season indicator — borough boards weigh in before the executive budget. March 19 at 8:30 AM.
Borough President - Manhattan | Public Hearings and Meetings

item.title: DYCD — Five Evaluation Task Orders to Policy Studies Associates
item.description:
Five $200K task orders for evaluations of SYEP, NDA, Train & Earn, Case Management, and Financial Literacy programs. One firm evaluating multiple flagship youth programs — worth watching for evaluation independence. Comments due March 20.
Youth and Community Development | Public Comment on Contract Awards | $1.0M

item.title: DOF Proposed Amendment of Rules Relating to Utility Company Properties
item.description:
Finance is proposing new or amended rules. Agency rule changes represent policy shifts that can affect how city services are delivered and regulated.
Finance | Agency Rules

item.title: Paper, Multipurpose
item.description:
Citywide Administrative Services is awarding a $11.0M contract to WB Mason Co Inc. This is one of the larger contracts in today's Record.
Citywide Administrative Services | Procurement | $11.0M

item.description:

item.description:

item.description:

Youth and Community Development | Procurement | $2.0M

Youth and Community Development | Procurement | $5.1M

item.description:

item.title: FY26 RFP Fatherhood Implementation
item.description:
Youth and Community Development is awarding a $4.0M contract to New York Foundling. Significant public expenditure.
Youth and Community Development | Procurement | $4.0M
